summary: Matched and improved parts of sysdolphin baselib texpdag by reshaping generated pointer-walking code into indexed loops, replacing split scalar clear data with a typed HSD_TEArg struct object, recovering a switch-shaped SimplifyThis region, and updating GALE01 symbol metadata for .data/.sdata including jump table alignment and string labels.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source change for order_dag, HSD_TExpMakeDag, HSD_TExpSchedule, SimplifySrc, SimplifyThis, typed clear argument data, loops, and switch recovery.
Symbol metadata updates for texpdag .data/.sdata, including splitting HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0 into lbl_* entries, adding jumptable align:8, and representing HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 as one 8-byte object.

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded 5 new matches: texpdag .data, SimplifyThis, SimplifySrc, .sdata, and order_dag; HSD_TExpMakeDag improved from 82.04% to 92.07%.
order_dag was simplified from pointer/counter temporaries into direct indexed access: blocked = ready_set | dep_bits and full_dep[i].

HSD_TExpMakeDag was heavily refactored from pointer-walking, gotos, manual unrolled initialization, and extra locals into indexed for-loops over c_in/a_in and sp94/sp14 arrays, while retaining PAD_STACK(4).
HSD_TExpSchedule changed args from static int args[8] to args[5] while retaining four initializer entries.

Two scalar globals at 0x804D5FF8 and 0x804D5FFC were replaced by one typed static HSD_TEArg HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 = { 0x00, 0x07, 0xFF }, and symbols.txt changed the sdata object size from two 4-byte entries to one 8-byte entry.
SimplifySrc switched from HSD_TEArg pointer variables and raw u32 writes to indexed c_in[i]/a_in[i] assignments and HSD_TEArg struct copies; result became bool-style true/false.
CLEAR_ARG was reduced from copying separate u32 and exp fields to assigning the HSD_TEArg clear object directly.
SimplifyThis replaced a deeply nested scalar-comparison region with a switch over arg0->tev.a_op and indexed accesses to a_in[i]/c_in[i].
symbols.txt split a former HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0 0x78-byte global object into several lbl_80407AA0..lbl_80407B04 entries and marked jumptable_80407B18 as local align:8.

smart_moves.text: Used the real aggregate type HSD_TEArg for the clear argument constant rather than forcing separate scalar globals, which explained both instruction ordering and sdata placement.
Recovered indexed array access over tev.c_in and tev.a_in instead of preserving generated pointer arithmetic through HSD_TEArg-sized object stepping.
Used a switch form for a deeply nested scalar-comparison region in SimplifyThis, aligning the code with the author's stated source-shape hypothesis.
Verified jump table/data correctness with objdiff's aggressive 'Name or address, data value' diffing while cross-checking assembly labels for not-yet-linked jump labels.
Kept uncertainty visible for HSD_TExpMakeDag rather than overclaiming the refactor as trustworthy source reconstruction.

decomp_lessons.lesson: If instruction order or data placement looks strange around adjacent scalars, test whether the original source used one typed aggregate; a contiguous initialized struct can keep a zero-valued trailing field in initialized data rather than BSS-like storage.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 symbols and source hunks

decomp_lessons.lesson: Deeply nested scalar comparisons may be decompiler output for a switch statement; consider switch recovery, including non-jumptable switch codegen, before preserving the comparison ladder.
An incrementing array index may compile into a forward-marching pointer dereference, so pointer-walking decompiler output can often be written as ordinary indexed array access.
Struct assignment can replace raw field-by-field or word-plus-pointer copies when the target field has a recovered struct type.
When a refactor improves matching but lacks a close match and risks index/increment mistakes, preserve that uncertainty and consider restarting from decomp.py for a future pass.

naming_conventions.text: Unproven data fragments in symbols.txt were named with address-style lbl_80407AA0/lbl_80407AAC/etc. rather than a semantic aggregate name.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt HSD_TExpDag_80407AA0 split into lbl_* entries

naming_conventions.text: Typed source retained address-style global HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 while changing its C type to HSD_TEArg; the PR evidence supports type recovery more strongly than semantic renaming.
Temporary/generated-style locals such as cur, cur2, p, loop_count3, loop_count4, clear_arg, and clear_exp were removed or replaced with scoped role locals and direct indexed fields.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For jump table data labels, compare objdiff and assembly views: objdiff may show unresolved '????' labels, while asm has useful labels; use aggressive 'Name or address, data value' diffing to validate data correctness.
Recovering a contiguous struct object can affect both instruction scheduling and data-section placement for initialized/zero-valued adjacent fields.
Try natural indexed loops over fixed-size arrays when generated output steps through a struct by adding sizeof(HSD_TEArg).
Try switch statements for branch ladders over scalar operation codes; m2c may not reconstruct this source shape automatically.
PAD_STACK(4) remained in HSD_TExpMakeDag; PR evidence shows it was retained but does not include a standalone justification beyond the diff context.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Jump tables are finicky; useful workflow was flipping between objdiff and asm, then verifying data with aggressive 'Name or address, data value' diffing.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 being a struct, not separate scalars, explains weird instruction order and why the following zero-looking data should remain contiguous with initialized data.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Deeply nested scalar comparisons usually hint at a switch statement; m2c may miss switch recovery.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Struct copy made the SimplifySrc/CLEAR_ARG cleanup easier; a leftover helper/shape might be removable in a future less-constrained diff.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: The HSD_TExpMakeDag improvement was not fully trusted; future work may be better started from scratch with decomp.py because index/increment mistakes are easy.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Array indexing on an incrementing counter can compile as a dereference of a forward-marching pointer.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Objdiff hover can show offsets from the function base for unresolved labels, but the author still preferred asm for lower friction and copy/paste workflows.

follow_up_queries: jump table objdiff ???? asm labels Name or address data value
m2c scalar comparisons switch statement recovery
HSD_TEArg array indexing forward marching pointer

notes: PR body is empty, so intent comes from diff and review comments.
Loaded raw diff and review comments are truncated; the record only uses visible hunks and metadata.
Bot report provides matching improvement evidence, but no local build or objdiff run was performed during intake.
Several comments are self-review notes by the PR author; they are evidence of technique and uncertainty, not independent reviewer acceptance.
HSD_TExpMakeDag was explicitly described by the author as improved but not fully trusted, so related lessons are framed as caution rather than accepted correctness.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Struct aggregate can explain scalar-looking data and section placement
accepted_candidate_records.text: In texpdag, replacing separate scalar globals at 0x804D5FF8/0x804D5FFC with one HSD_TEArg object explained unusual instruction ordering and why a zero-looking trailing value stayed contiguous with initialized sdata.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 source and symbols hunks

accepted_candidate_records.title: Generated pointer walks may be indexed array loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In SimplifyThis/SimplifySrc, HSD_TEArg-sized pointer stepping over c_in/a_in was rewritten as arg0->tev.c_in[i] and arg0->tev.a_in[i]; the author noted MWCC can compile incrementing array indexing into a forward-marching pointer dereference.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use asm alongside objdiff for unresolved jump table labels
accepted_candidate_records.text: For jumptable_80407B18, the author found objdiff useful but noted unresolved labels display as '????'; assembly labels and the aggressive 'Name or address, data value' diffing type helped verify the data match.

source_update_candidates.title: Document struct aggregate recovery for scalar-looking adjacent data
source_update_candidates.text: When adjacent scalar-looking data has odd instruction ordering or section-placement pressure, test whether it is a single typed aggregate. PR #2757 shows HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8/HSD_TExpDag_804D5FFC becoming one HSD_TEArg object, explaining contiguous initialized sdata despite a zero-valued trailing field.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 source and symbols hunks
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially reusable beyond this PR, but curator should decide whether it belongs in global standards or as a narrower lesson.

source_update_candidates.title: Add jump table matching workflow note
source_update_candidates.text: For jump table data, objdiff may show unresolved '????' labels while asm contains useful labels; compare both views and consider aggressive 'Name or address, data value' diffing to validate symbol/data matches.
source_update_candidates.reason: The workflow is concrete and evidenced, but should be curated as a tactic rather than treated as a mandatory standard.

source_update_candidates.title: texpdag uses HSD_TEArg arrays and switch-like TEV simplification patterns
source_update_candidates.text: In src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c, c_in/a_in fixed-size HSD_TEArg arrays and TEV op simplification code were successfully matched using indexed loops, struct copies, and switch recovery.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future work in this file, but should remain path-scoped because evidence is local to texpdag.

source_update_candidates.title: HSD_TExpMakeDag remained partially uncertain after PR #2757
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2757 improved HSD_TExpMakeDag matching but the author explicitly did not fully trust the refactor and suggested restarting from decomp.py for future work because wrong array indices or dropped increments are easy.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful caution for future agents touching this function; should be curated as a risk/path note, not as a correctness claim.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that every nested comparison ladder is a switch; the evidence supports using it as a hint and testing switch forms.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(4) in HSD_TExpMakeDag as a proven reusable tactic from this PR; the diff shows it remained, but no comment justifies it.
Do not infer semantic names for lbl_80407AA0 or related labels; PR evidence only supports address-style splitting and data classification.
Do not promote HSD_TExpMakeDag as fully matched or trusted; bot report says it improved to 92.07%, and the author explicitly expressed low trust.
